variants.title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the lightning flash behind this animal behavior: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
variants.seo_title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
variants.yt_description: Lightning makes air explode into thunder. Watch the lightning flash first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Lightning turns air into a shock wave.
variants.script: Lightning turns air into a shock wave. Watch the flash first, because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast. That air expands suddenly, and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder. The sound is the atmosphere snapping back. Which storm signal next?

variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the wing scales behind this animal behavior: Butterflies are covered in tiny color scales
variants.seo_title: Butterflies carry color on tiny wing scales
variants.yt_description: Butterflies carry color on tiny wing scales. Watch the wing scales first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Butterflies are covered in tiny color scales.

variants.title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the tongue smell behind this animal behavior: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.seo_title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.yt_description: Snakes smell the air with their tongues. Watch the tongue smell first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick.
variants.script: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?

variants.title: Lions use dark ear marks to guide cubs
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the ear marks behind this animal behavior: Lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow
variants.seo_title: Lions use dark ear marks to guide cubs
variants.yt_description: Lions use dark ear marks to guide cubs. Watch the ear marks first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow.
variants.script: Lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow. Watch the backs of the ears, because those dark marks can stand out when a lion moves through grass. For cubs, that contrast helps track the adult ahead. The detail is small, but the pride depends on staying together. Would you spot it?
